key_insight: The paper introduces a multi-scale machine learning framework that jointly models chemical, spin, and structural disorder in alloys to predict thermodynamic phase behavior.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable design and screening of complex magnetic alloys, including high-entropy and spintronic materials, without relying solely on expensive trial-and-error simulations or experiments.
method: Couples graph neural networks and machine-learning interatomic potentials with Monte Carlo and molecular dynamics.
Captures configurational disorder (chemical/spin) together with local lattice distortions from interstitials.
Applies the framework to bcc Fe-Co-C as a prototypical multi-disorder alloy.
evidence_signal: The framework predicts the Fe-Co order-to-disorder transition at 1,000 K and melting at 1,690 K, closely matching experimental values of 1,006 K and about 1,700 K, and also reproduces tetragonal-to-nearly-cubic transitions in Fe-Co-C.
watch_item: Accuracy depends on the quality and transferability of the trained ML models and potentials across temperature, composition, and disorder regimes not explicitly covered in training.
paper_title: A Multi-Scale Machine Learning Framework for Coupled Chemical, Spin, and Structural Disorder in Alloys
